tasks.subject: [connections-tab-ux-improvements] [P2] Replace empty state with action-focused CTA
tasks.description:
## Objective Replace the passive "No adapters configured yet." text in the Relay ConnectionsTab with a visually rich empty state containing a muted icon, description text, and a pointer to the Available Adapters section below. ## File to Modify ### `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ConnectionsTab.tsx` Replace the empty state paragraph (line 103-104 in current code): **Before:** ```tsx

No adapters configured yet.

``` **After:** ```tsx import { Plug2 } from 'lucide-react'; // ... inside the configuredCards.length === 0 branch:

No adapters configured

Add an adapter below to connect agents to external services

``` The text points downward to the "Available Adapters" section which already has per-adapter "Add" buttons. No separate CTA button is needed — this avoids duplicate CTAs and keeps the empty state minimal per Calm Tech design principles. ## Test Update Update the existing test in `ConnectionsTab.test.tsx` that checks for empty state text: ```tsx it('renders empty state with icon and description when no adapters configured', () => { mockUseAdapterCatalog.mockReturnValue({ data: [{ manifest: telegramManifest, instances: [] }], isLoading: false }); render(, { wrapper }); expect(screen.getByText('No adapters configured')).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByText('Add an adapter below to connect agents to external services')).toBeInTheDocument(); }); ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Empty state shows Plug2 icon (muted, size-8) - [ ] Empty state shows "No adapters configured" primary text - [ ] Empty state shows "Add an adapter below to connect agents to external services" secondary text - [ ] Empty state has rounded-xl, border-dashed, centered layout with py-8 - [ ] No "Add Adapter" button in the empty state (CTA is the Available Adapters section below) - [ ] Test verifies both text strings are rendered
tasks.activeForm: Replacing empty state with action-focused CTA

tasks.subject: [connections-tab-ux-improvements] [P2] Replace fixed grid-cols-2 with responsive auto-fill grid
tasks.description:
## Objective

Replace the hardcoded `grid-cols-2` on the Available Adapters CatalogCard grid with CSS `auto-fill` so the grid is responsive to panel width — 1 column in narrow sidebar mode, 2 columns when space allows.

## File to Modify

### `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ConnectionsTab.tsx`

**Before (line 131):**
```tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
```

**After:**
```tsx
<div className="grid gap-2" style={{ gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(auto-fill, minmax(240px, 1fr))' }}>
```

The inline style is used because Tailwind v4 does not have a built-in utility for `auto-fill` with `minmax`. This produces 1 column when the panel is under ~500px wide (typical sidebar) and 2 columns when space allows. The configured adapter list (AdapterCard stack) keeps its single-column `space-y-3` layout — no change needed there.

Also update the skeleton loading state to use the same responsive grid (line 42-43 in current code):

**Before:**
```tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-3">
```

**After:**
```tsx
<div className="grid gap-3" style={{ gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(auto-fill, minmax(240px, 1fr))' }}>
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CatalogCard grid uses `auto-fill` with `minmax(240px, 1fr)`
- [ ] Skeleton loading grid matches the same responsive layout
- [ ] Single-column layout at panel widths under ~500px
- [ ] Two-column layout when panel width exceeds ~500px
- [ ] No changes to the configured adapter card layout (remains single-column stack)
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
tasks.activeForm: Replacing fixed grid with responsive auto-fill grid
